diagram_spec:
graph G {
  layout=neato
  graph [bgcolor="#0d1117"]
  node [style="filled,rounded" fillcolor="#161b22" color="#30363d" fontcolor="#e6edf3" fontsize=13 shape=box]
  edge [color="#8b949e" fontcolor="#8b949e" fontsize=10]
  Interface [label="Interface" fontsize=16 color="#00b4d8"]
  Abstract [label="Abstract Class"]
  Interface -- Abstract [label="vs"]
}
analogy: Interfaces are like a contract, while abstract classes are like a blueprint

concept: Default and Static Methods in Interfaces
diagram_spec:
graph G {
  layout=neato
  graph [bgcolor="#0d1117"]
  node [style="filled,rounded" fillcolor="#161b22" color="#30363d" fontcolor="#e6edf3" fontsize=13 shape=box]
  edge [color="#8b949e" fontcolor="#8b949e" fontsize=10]
  Interface [label="Interface" fontsize=16 color="#00b4d8"]
  Default [label="Default Method"]
  Static [label="Static Method"]
  Interface -- Default [label="has"]
  Interface -- Static [label="owns"]
}
analogy: Default methods are like a fallback, while static methods are like a utility

hook_question: Can a class implement multiple interfaces?
key_points: A class can implement multiple interfaces
Only one class can be extended
diagram_spec:
graph G {
  layout=neato
  graph [bgcolor="#0d1117"]
  node [style="filled,rounded" fillcolor="#161b22" color="#30363d" fontcolor="#e6edf3" fontsize=13 shape=box]
  edge [color="#8b949e" fontcolor="#8b949e" fontsize=10]
  Class [label="Class" fontsize=16 color="#00b4d8"]
  Interface1 [label="Interface 1"]
  Interface2 [label="Interface 2"]
  Class -- Interface1 [label="implements"]
  Class -- Interface2 [label="implements"]

analogy: Implementing multiple interfaces is like wearing multiple hats

diagram_spec:
graph G {
  layout=neato
  graph [bgcolor="#0d1117"]
  node [style="filled,rounded" fillcolor="#161b22" color="#30363d" fontcolor="#e6edf3" fontsize=13 shape=box]
  edge [color="#8b949e" fontcolor="#8b949e" fontsize=10]
  Small [label="Small Interface" fontsize=16 color="#00b4d8"]
  Focused [label="Focused Interface"]
  Small -- Focused [label="is"]
}
analogy: Interfaces should be like a single-purpose tool, not a Swiss Army knife

memory_hooks: Interfaces are for 'can do', abstract classes are for 'is a'
Default methods have a body, static methods don't belong to the instance
A class can implement multiple interfaces, but only extend one class
Interfaces should be small and focused, like a single-purpose tool
